prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap of pyridine; units eV.
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis (no density fitting) – written as B3LYP/def2-TZVP.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz; no further geometry optimization (notation: DFT//user-provided geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: PySCF (backend invoked by chemkit frontier-orbitals skill via cli: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier …).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 10 cycles; no warnings were reported by the engine. The live SCF/SCF log was streamed to frontier_<timestamp>.out in the working directory (path echoed at run start). The final structured result is saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpj4zrvry4/live.json.

Results (Koopmans-style orbital energies, negatives indicate bound states):
• HOMO energy  = –7.2525992 eV
• LUMO energy  = –1.112786 eV
• HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV

These orbital energies come directly from the converged B3LYP/def2-TZVP Kohn–Sham eigenvalues and represent vertical ionization (IP) and electron-affinity (EA) estimates under the Koopmans approximation; they are *not* corrected excitation energies.

Reproducibility details: see the cli_invocation string in the JSON for the exact command, the `.xyz` geometry path above, the live `.out` log (written during the run to the working directory, available for inspection with `tail -f`), and the full result JSON path noted. Together these artifacts allow the calculation to be rerun or audited without ambiguity.

No warnings were reported; integrity.trustworthy = true.
